heading: Prereview of television advertisements
label: 353c Prereview of television advertisements
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to renumbering by .section 353b of this titlePub. L. 113–54
Effective DateSection effective 180 days after , see , set out as an Effective Date of 2007 Amendment note under .Sept. 27, 2007section 909 of Pub. L. 110–85section 331 of this title
amendments: Effective DateSection effective 180 days after , see , set out as an Effective Date of 2007 Amendment note under .Sept. 27, 2007section 909 of Pub. L. 110–85section 331 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary may require the submission of any television advertisement for a drug (including any script, story board, rough, or a completed video production of the television advertisement) to the Secretary for review under this section not later than 45 days before dissemination of the television advertisement.

children.label: A
children.text: necessary to protect the consumer good and well-being; or

children.label: B
children.text: consistent with prescribing information for the product under review; and

if appropriate and if information exists, on statements for inclusion in the advertisement to address the specific efficacy of the drug as it relates to specific population groups, including elderly populations, children, and racial and ethnic minorities.

children.heading: No authority to require changes
children.label: c No authority to require changes
children.text: Except as provided by subsection (e), this section does not authorize the Secretary to make or direct changes in any material submitted pursuant to subsection (a).

children.heading: Elderly populations, children, racially and ethnically diverse communities
children.label: d Elderly populations, children, racially and ethnically diverse communities
children.text: In formulating recommendations under subsection (b), the Secretary shall take into consideration the impact of the advertised drug on elderly populations, children, and racially and ethnically diverse communities.

children.heading: Serious risk; safety protocol
children.label: 1 Serious risk; safety protocol
children.text: In conducting a review of a television advertisement under this section, if the Secretary determines that the advertisement would be false or misleading without a specific disclosure about a serious risk listed in the labeling of the drug involved, the Secretary may require inclusion of such disclosure in the advertisement.

children.heading: Date of approval
children.label: 2 Date of approval
children.text: In conducting a review of a television advertisement under this section, the Secretary may require the advertisement to include, for a period not to exceed 2 years from the date of the approval of the drug under or , a specific disclosure of such date of approval if the Secretary determines that the advertisement would otherwise be false or misleading.section 355 of this titlesection 262 of title 42

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: f Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this section may be construed as having any effect on requirements under or on the authority of the Secretary under section 314.550, 314.640, 601.45, or 601.94 of title 21, Code of Federal Regulations (or successor regulations).section 352(n) of this title
